days.horoscope:
Today, the path becomes clearer. You may feel called toward something specific—a project, a conversation, a new direction—something that feels like movement.

Trust this.

This is a day to walk forward with purpose, knowing you’re supported both physically and spiritually. Even if you feel alone, you’re part of a larger flow of people growing, evolving, and making meaning in their own ways.

Follow the signs. Follow the pull. Let momentum guide you.
days.affirmation: I walk my path with purpose, clarity, and the support of those who walk beside me.
days.meditation:
Close your eyes and picture a long glowing path stretching between two overlapping cities—one shadowed, one luminous. Walk toward it.

As you step onto the path, see soft figures of light traveling with you. Some ahead, some behind, some beside. Feel their presence as gentle support, a community of souls moving toward their own destinations.

Walk until the path rises into a bridge. Pause in the middle. Look across the cities merging below—both the broken parts you’ve healed and the luminous parts you’re growing into.

Feel your next step form clearly in your heart.

When you are ready, cross the rest of the bridge. Open your eyes knowing your journey is shared, and your direction true.

birthday.title: The Guided Pilgrim
birthday.content:
To be born on 10 E' is to be a leader on the path of life. You possess a restless spirit (E') that is grounded by a deep sense of purpose and responsibility (Ten). You are not a wanderer who gets lost; you are an explorer who draws the map for others. You have a natural authority and a talent for guiding groups, whether on physical journeys or spiritual ones. People follow you because you seem to know exactly where you are going.

Your life path is one of manifestation through movement. You learn by doing and by going. You may have a career that involves travel, trade, or diplomacy. Your challenge is to not become too rigid in your itinerary. The road has twists and turns. Your lesson is to remain open to the detour while keeping your eyes on the destination. You are here to show us that the journey is real and that we can walk it together.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for E' (Path/Tooth/Head) glowing with internal road-dust-white and sky-blue light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 10: two glowing golden bars arranged horizontally. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

days.chapter:
The platform at the end of the bridge narrows into a quiet residential district—or what remains of one. The buildings here stand straighter. Their windows glow with soft, steady light, not flickering or fractured. Vines from the forest district curl along balconies, and patches of the luminous city shine through the walls like gentle lanterns.

The air feels different. Still, but full.

As you walk, you notice a repeating structure appearing at regular intervals: tall stone columns embedded into the streets and doorways. Some are cracked, others polished smooth by time, but each one hums with a grounding presence. The sound is subtle, a deep resonance that seems to settle your breath and lengthen your spine.

You approach one of the pillars.

Its surface glows with faint etched patterns—lines that look like roots and constellations intertwined. When you place your hand on it, a pulse rises through your arm, warm and steady.

Something inside you responds.

Memories of every place you’ve called home flicker through your mind: rooms you outgrew, corners where you hid, the one place you left too quickly, the places where you stayed too long. You feel the weight of all those temporary shelters, all the times you kept moving because stillness felt dangerous.

The column hums louder—not demanding, not summoning, but acknowledging.

A gentle shift spreads through the district. The buildings straighten further. Doorframes realign. Even the air feels more centered. You understand now: these columns aren’t remnants.

They’re anchors.

As you continue walking, more columns appear in your path, each one supporting the quiet architecture of this shifting world. Some glow gold. Others emit soft blue or gentle violet. Each carries a unique rhythm, but all of them share one purpose: to hold the world steady.

Then you reach the heart of the district.

A circular plaza holds a massive pillar—wider than a tree trunk, tall enough to merge with the twilight sky. Its surface is smooth stone, but alive with light moving through faint carved pathways. You step closer and feel your body settle in a way you haven’t known for years.

This pillar feels like a spine.

Not of a building or a city—of you.

You place both hands on it. The resonance deepens, spreading through your bones. Strength rises quietly, not in the form of power or confidence, but in simple presence. Your breath becomes slower, fuller. You feel your entire body align, rooted yet open.

A truth moves through you:

You do not always have to travel.

You do not always have to run.

There is a home inside you that cannot be destroyed.

For the first time, the city is not just guiding you.

It is welcoming you.

number.title: The Force of Dissolution
number.content: Eleven is the number of dissonance, change, and the breaking of patterns. It is the energy that disrupts the status quo to clear the way for higher order. Eleven tests the structures we have built. Experientially, an Eleven day can feel chaotic or liberating. It is the loose brick in the wall. The shadow of Eleven is overwhelm. It asks us to be flexible and to find freedom in letting things fall apart.

nawal.title: The Pillar of Home
nawal.content: Aj is the nawal of the cane, the home, and the spine. It represents authority, leadership, and the staff of power. Aj is the energy of stability, family, and community integrity. It governs the spinal column and the home environment. In its shadow, Aj can manifest as rigidity, authoritarianism, or family problems. Its highest expression is the upright leader who supports the people like a strong pillar.

combined_energy.title: Shaking the Pillars
combined_energy.content: Today, the disruptive force of Eleven meets the stabilizing energy of Aj. This is a day of 'Structural Testing' or 'Family Realignment.' The Eleven shakes the pillars of the home and the community to see if they are truly sound. It is a day where family secrets may come out, or where rigid authority figures are challenged. The energy can feel uncomfortable, as our sources of security (Aj) are being tested (Eleven). However, this is necessary for growth. If a structure survives the shaking of the Eleven, you know it is true. Use this day to examine the 'rules' of your house or your life. Are they supporting you, or are they constraining you? Be willing to let go of outdated traditions or rigid power dynamics. The goal is a home that is flexible enough to withstand the storm, not a fortress that cracks under pressure.
notes: Check the physical structure of your home (spine) for issues.
Have an honest conversation about family dynamics.
Release a rigid belief about how life 'should' be.
Stretch your spine; practice yoga for flexibility.
Stand tall in your own truth, even if it shakes the room.

days.horoscope:
Today asks you to root into yourself even as life moves in multiple directions. You may feel pulled toward different tasks, emotions, or choices—but underneath it all, there is a steady pulse guiding you.

This is a day for grounding, for choosing presence over speed, for remembering that your strength doesn’t come from doing everything perfectly. It comes from standing in your truth like a pillar.

Let yourself slow down. Let yourself breathe. Let yourself feel the quiet stability rising inside you.

From that center, you can handle anything.
days.affirmation: I am rooted, steady, and supported by the quiet strength within me.
days.meditation:
Sit comfortably and close your eyes. Imagine walking through a peaceful district of the twilight city where tall glowing pillars rise between buildings.

See yourself approach a central pillar—a massive structure of stone and light. Place your hands upon it. Feel the warm resonance travel into your palms, up your arms, and through your spine.

With each breath, feel your body align. Feel your energy settle. Imagine roots extending gently from your feet into the ground, anchoring you in calm strength.

Let this pillar represent the home inside you—the place that cannot be shaken. Sit with its grounding presence for a few moments.

When you are ready, open your eyes, carrying that steadiness forward.

birthday.title: The Reformer of Tradition
birthday.content:
To be born on 11 Aj is to be a challenger of the established order within the home and community. You have a deep love for family and tradition (Aj), but the number Eleven makes you a non-conformist who cannot abide by rules that don't make sense. You are the 'black sheep' who ends up healing the family line. You see where the cracks are in the foundation and you aren't afraid to point them out. You have a natural authority, but it is a revolutionary authority.

Your life path is one of redefining what 'home' and 'leadership' mean. You may experience upheaval in your early life that forces you to build your own sense of stability from the inside out. Your challenge is to not simply destroy for the sake of destruction. Your lesson is to use your power to renovate, not demolish. You are here to build a new kind of home—one that is strong enough to hold the truth.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Aj (Cornstalk/Staff) glowing with internal golden-yellow and woody-brown light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 11: two glowing golden bars with one glowing dot above them. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

days.chapter:
You leave the pillar district with a steadiness that feels new. The streets welcome you back with softer rhythms, the twilight gentler than before. As you walk, the boundaries between the two cities—the broken and the luminous—begin to blur more noticeably.

Cracks in the pavement glow faintly with green-gold light. Moss forms intricate spirals along the walls. Windows shimmer with reflections not your own: glimpses of forests, mountains, waterfalls, starfields. The city feels alive today, breathing in long, patient cycles.

Soon, you enter a part of the city where the buildings melt seamlessly into nature. Tree roots braid into staircases. Stone merges with living bark. Ferns unfurl from between glowing tiles. Soft mist drifts through archways formed from intertwined branches and steel.

You sense a presence—not watching you, but observing the city through you.

The air thickens with a low, velvety hum. Light gathers at the edges of the path in subtle geometric patterns. For a moment, the world becomes quieter than quiet.

Then you see it.

A jaguar-shaped silhouette, made not of fur but of shifting twilight colors—deep indigo, soft gold, muted green—stepping from behind a massive root structure. Its body glows faintly, as if touched by starlight. Its eyes are luminous pools of calm intelligence.

You freeze, but the jaguar’s presence is not dangerous. It is ancient. Sacred. Protective.

It doesn’t approach. It simply walks beside you, mirroring your pace. Wherever it steps, the path brightens—small flowers opening in its footsteps, stones humming with soft resonance. The jaguar’s breath is slow, grounded, steady.

You feel your senses sharpen.

You hear the heartbeat of the city.

You smell distant rain.

You feel energy rising through the ground like warm, living current.

For a moment, you understand the city not as two fractured layers, but as a single living spirit—vast, interconnected, aware.

The jaguar pauses before a stone archway covered in vines. You step toward it, and the vines bloom instantly with pale, glowing flowers. The air shifts into stillness again.

The jaguar meets your gaze.

Not threatening, not pleading—only revealing.

And you understand:

Magic didn’t arrive here today.

It was always present.

You’re only now clear enough to feel it.

When you pass through the archway, the jaguar dissolves into drifting motes of gold and green that scatter into the twilight.

The city breathes with you.

Every corner feels enchanted. Every sound feels intentional. Every movement feels part of a pattern older than you, older than any city.

You belong here now, not as a wanderer—but as someone who can finally sense the unseen threads that hold the world together.

number.title: The Crystal of Understanding
number.content: Twelve is the number of understanding, summation, and the gathering of scattered parts into a whole. It represents the family, the tribe, and complex stability. Twelve is the 'council' number—it holds space for all previous experiences to be integrated. Experientially, a Twelve day feels rich and cooperative. It is a time to look back and see the lesson. The shadow of Twelve can be heaviness. It asks us to find unity in diversity.

nawal.title: The Jaguar of Magic
nawal.content: Ix is the nawal of the jaguar, the earth, and high magic. It represents the feminine power of nature and the mysteries of the night. Ix is the energy of the shaman and the healer. It governs the shrines and the sacred places of the earth. Ix energy is stealthy, powerful, and intuitive. In its shadow, Ix can be manipulative or secretive. Its highest expression is the wise use of spiritual power to heal and protect.

combined_energy.title: The Council of Earth Magic
combined_energy.content: When the integrative understanding of Twelve meets the earth magic of Ix, we have a day of 'Collective Magic' or 'Deep Wisdom.' The scattered energies of the trecena are now gathered into a powerful, magical whole. This is a day where the veil is thin and the earth is alive. It is a perfect day for group ceremonies, healing circles, or connecting with nature on a deep level. The Twelve brings a sense of community to the solitary jaguar, suggesting that our magic is stronger when we share it. You may feel a deep sense of understanding about the mysteries of your life—why things happened the way they did. The energy is rich, feminine, and grounding. The challenge is to hold the complexity; life is not simple, but it is magical. Trust your intuition (the jaguar) and lean on your community (the twelve).
notes: Visit a sacred site or a place of natural beauty with friends.
Perform a gratitude ceremony for Mother Earth.
Trust your intuition in social situations; you can see the unseen.
Reflect on the 'magic' of everyday life.
Send healing energy to the planet.

days.horoscope:
Today brings subtle revelations—the quiet kind that settle into your bones rather than flash in your mind. You may sense deeper meaning in ordinary moments, or feel more connected to your surroundings.

This is intuition waking fully. Earth magic rising. Inner clarity gathering strength.

Trust what you feel, even if you can’t explain it. Notice the signs that appear around you. This is a day to walk gently, listen closely, and let the world speak to you in its own language.
days.affirmation: I honor the unseen magic in my life and trust the wisdom rising within me.
days.meditation:
Close your eyes and imagine stepping into a part of the twilight city where buildings and forest blend into one living landscape.

See a jaguar-shaped being made of twilight light walking beside you. Feel its steady presence, its grounded steps, its deep awareness.

As you walk together, watch flowers bloom where it steps. Feel your senses sharpen—every sound clearer, every color richer.

The jaguar stops at a vine-covered archway. You step through it. Behind you, the jaguar dissolves into golden-green light.

Stand in the stillness that follows. Feel the earth beneath you humming with quiet magic.

When you are ready, open your eyes, carrying that subtle awareness forward.

birthday.title: The Earth Wisdom Keeper
birthday.content:
To be born on 12 Ix is to be a powerful conduit for earth magic and community healing. You possess a deep, intuitive wisdom (Ix) backed by a broad understanding of human nature (Twelve). You are likely a healer, a counselor, or an environmentalist—someone who works to restore the balance between people and the planet. You have a mysterious, magnetic quality that draws people to you, but you also love your solitude. You are the 'village witch' or the 'wise elder' archetype, regardless of your age.

Your life path is one of integrating the spiritual and the social. You are not meant to practice your magic in a cave; you are meant to bring it into the council circle. Your challenge is to manage your sensitivity. You feel the pain of the earth and the people deeply. Your lesson is to use your magic not to escape reality, but to transform it. You are here to remind us that the earth is sacred and that we are all connected.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Ix (Jaguar/Spots) glowing with internal jungle-green and amber light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 12: two glowing golden bars with two glowing dots above them. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

days.chapter:
You feel it before you see it.

A widening inside your chest. A soft pressure behind your eyes. A sense that the city—both the broken version and the luminous one—has stretched itself to make room for something vast.

You follow an upward current in the air, a pull gentle but insistent. Staircases appear where none existed before, spiraling along the outside of tall structures. Walkways extend like invitations. Platforms open like petals.

You climb.

With every level, the horizon expands. The twilight sky brightens into soft gradients of rose and amber, the first true hint of something like dawn. The higher you rise, the more the two cities blend—cracked stone glowing with golden seams, luminous towers showing faint outlines of rust beneath their radiance.

At the top of the final staircase, you reach an open terrace.

Wind moves through it—not cold, not harsh, but clean. Clearing.

The terrace is empty except for a single structure at its edge: a tall, delicate lattice of metal and light shaped like an unfinished spire. Its geometric patterns shift slowly, catching the rising glow of the sky.

You approach it, sensing that this is the highest place in the city—the point where vision is possible.

The sky changes.

A ripple of gold crosses the horizon. Then another. Feathers of light begin forming in the air, drifting gently until they spiral upward. They gather above the spire, weaving themselves into the shape of a great bird—vast, luminous, made of shifting gold and shimmering twilight blue.

An eagle.

It doesn’t fully manifest. It is half energy, half outline, wings made of living light. But its presence is unmistakable. You feel it in your bones.

The eagle doesn’t descend. It lifts.

And when it rises, your vision rises with it.

Suddenly you see everything:

The shadow-city with its broken structures and memory-haunted streets.
The luminous city with its soft gold, gentle lines, and living architecture.
The forest district, the weaving plaza, the glowing path, the rooftop altar.
Every place you walked.

Every step you took.

Every moment you thought you were lost.

From above, nothing is broken.

Nothing is separate.

Everything belongs.

The eagle arcs through the air, scattering feathers of gold that drift down like blessings. One lands near your feet and dissolves into light, sending a warm surge up your spine and into your chest. It feels like gratitude. Like recognition. Like release.

When you look up again, the eagle has risen into the brightening sky.

The city exhales.

You realize the trecena has carried you from the first crack of light into full vision—not a final destination, but a perspective wide enough to see where you might go next.

Everything feels possible.

Everything feels open.

And for the first time, you are ready for whatever the next dawn brings.

number.title: The Light of Ascension
number.content: Thirteen is the number of completion, ascension, and the spirit world. It represents the final step in the trecena cycle—the return to source at a higher level. Thirteen is the number of the ancestors and the 'invisible army.' Experientially, a Thirteen day feels mystical and surreal. It is a day of wrapping up and letting go. The shadow of Thirteen can be ungroundedness. It asks us to celebrate the journey.

nawal.title: The Eagle of Vision
nawal.content: Tz'ikin is the nawal of the bird, the eagle, and vision. It represents prosperity, freedom, and the ability to see the big picture. Tz'ikin is the messenger between earth and sky. It governs abundance and business. Tz'ikin energy is optimistic, expansive, and high-flying. In its shadow, it can be distracted or greedy. Its highest expression is the visionary who manifests spiritual abundance for all.

combined_energy.title: Ascending Vision
combined_energy.content: We reach the culmination of the Aq'ab'al trecena with 13 Tz'ikin—the 'Ascended Vision' or 'Spiritual Abundance.' The cycle that began with the dawn of hope now ends with the high-flying view of the eagle. Today, you can see the entire journey clearly. The fog has lifted. The Thirteen elevates the prosperity of Tz'ikin to a spiritual level—it is a day of harvesting not just wealth, but wisdom and freedom. The energy is ecstatic, light, and expansive. It is a perfect day to dream big for the future, to give thanks for the abundance in your life, and to connect with the spirit world. The ancestors are celebrating with you. The challenge is to stay grounded enough to enjoy it. Don't just fly away; bring that eagle vision down to earth. Share your abundance. Realize that the dawn you hoped for on Day 1 has now become the full, bright sun of Day 13.
notes: Write down your vision for the next year; dream without limits.
Make a donation or share your wealth in some way.
Look at the sky; watch for birds as messengers.
Celebrate! Have a feast or a party to honor the completion of the cycle.
Say 'thank you' for everything—the light and the dark.

days.horoscope:
Today brings vision.

Not the kind that solves every problem at once, but the kind that widens your inner horizon. You may see connections that were invisible before, feel new clarity about your direction, or experience a sudden lift in your spirit.

This is a day for perspective—rising above doubt, confusion, and the noise of daily life to glimpse what truly matters.

Let yourself be inspired. Let yourself dream bigger. Let yourself see the world from a vantage point you’ve earned.
days.affirmation: I rise into clarity and welcome the vision that opens the next chapter of my life.
days.meditation:
Close your eyes and imagine standing on a high terrace in the twilight city. The horizon stretches wide, glowing with soft gold and deep blue.

A structure of light and metal rises beside you—a spire catching the first rays of a new dawn. Above it, feathers of golden light gather into the shape of a great eagle.

As the eagle lifts, feel your awareness lift with it. See the whole city from above: the broken places, the luminous places, the paths you walked, the moments of healing.

Let this wide vision settle into your heart.

When the eagle disappears into the brightening sky, breathe deeply and open your eyes, carrying that clarity with you.

birthday.title: The Cosmic Visionary
birthday.content:
To be born on 13 Tz'ikin is to be a master of manifestation and a seer of high destinies. You possess a limitless imagination (Tz'ikin) backed by the power of the spirit world (Thirteen). You are a person who can make things happen simply by envisioning them. You have a natural connection to abundance and often live a life of freedom and prosperity. People are drawn to your optimism and your ability to see the best in everyone.

Your life path is one of soaring above the mundane. You are not meant for smallness; you are meant for the sky. You may be a visionary entrepreneur, a spiritual teacher, or a philanthropist. Your challenge is to not lose touch with the ground. You can be so focused on the horizon that you trip over the stone in front of you. Your lesson is to bring your vision down to earth—to use your abundance to uplift others. You are here to show us that we are all meant to fly.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Tz'ikin (Eagle/Bird) glowing with internal sky-blue and prosperity-gold light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 13: two glowing golden bars with three glowing dots above them. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.
